reviews.author: Mathy0
reviews.text: Ticket is 185k per person, it's included all in the picture below. Very nice place and worth it to go! We used grab bike to get here and then we took a taxi of one othe people staying outside the attraction that offered to us. Water is beautiful

reviews.author: VIETNAMTOURPACKAGE
reviews.text: Ba Ho Eco is about 45 minutes away from Nha Trang City centre. This resort setting is fabulous with a holistic wellness concept as well as eco friendly. Basic room but convenient. There is the great water spring along with and nice pools for swimming. We will definitely come back to this paradise to enjoy quality time. ||* Weekdays is my best choice to avoid the local tourists groups to visit here. This site is more suitable for couples and families.

reviews.author: *Clawgey1852*
reviews.text: Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "A must-visit if you’re in the Nha Trang area and looking for more than just a beach day. Ba Hồ is stunning but be warned: this isn't a casual stroll! Reaching the 3rd lake is very challenging and involves some serious rock scrambling and climbing. ​The scenery is breathtaking and the freshwater pools are the perfect reward for the effort. If you enjoy physical activity and nature, you’ll love it. Wear good shoes with grip—flip-flops are a recipe for disaster here! Open 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM."

reviews.author: Peter Craig
reviews.text: Bring sturdy shoes or sandals that strap on securely to your feet to make the somewhat difficult trek to the 3rd waterfall. I'm 60 and did it fine in Teva's. A great outing for sure.

reviews.author: Joyce Tunc
reviews.text: We went there on a Sunday by motorbike. It was easy to find as there a many signs along the roads leading to the Ba Ho waterfall. Parking is free. You pay 185.000 dong per person and skywalk and kayak is included. You get a bottle of water per person. At the entry are shuttles who can bring you through the forest to the waterfall. We walked and it’s a beautiful clean path with toilets, trash bins and food or drink stands along the way. Beautiful atmosphere. The last part up to the waterfall is climbing up on huge rocks which are painted with arrows to lead the way. But you MUST have closed and kind of hiking shoes. It is a bit rough way and there are no ropes or sidebars to hold on. I would say you definitely need a bit better physical condition to do this. There were some younger people or old people who struggled to get up there to first level. We didn’t go further as it was enough adventure for me personally up to this level and it was about to rain. The water was not so high and it looked more like river with huge rocks and they were very slippery. Every person who got into water could not made it out selfly as there was just nothing to hold onto. I slipped a few times in the water due to massive stones all sizes which you couldn’t see as the water was not so clear. In the water many small fish trying to bite your foot and legs. That got me uncomfortable. Water was cold and refreshing, but nothing for beginners or totally inexperienced people. Because you can easily get hurt very badly. It’s a beautiful place and definitely a great atmosphere there and people having fun. But I would recommend that they put up some more ropes on the rocks to climb up and down the way or even at the lakes that you can get easier in and out the water.

reviews.author: iampetertran
reviews.text: One of my favorite places to visit while in Nha Trang to Escape the city with Good outdoors experiences, challenging hikes, Easy access. Has onsite resort, only 30 minutes from town.

reviews.author: Jacek
reviews.text: 27.09.25 Absolutely beautiful place that I highly recommend to everyone. Besides its natural charm, special praise goes to the lady selling tickets. Her patience in answering every question and the numerous valuable tips she gave us made our visit even more pleasant. Definitely wonderful service!

reviews.author: Sean
reviews.text: It's a good half day visit. The environment is very well maintained and presented. It is nice walk to the waterfall and a fun swim. The food wasn't the best, but ok. Well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Gholamreza Mohammady
reviews.text: Ba Hồ is an absolute hidden gem in Nha Trang! The hike through the jungle to reach the three waterfalls was both exciting and refreshing, with breathtaking views at every turn. The water was crystal clear and perfect for swimming, and the natural pools felt like a private paradise. There were also adventurous options like cliff jumping and ziplining for thrill-seekers. The area was well-maintained, with friendly staff and clean facilities. It’s a perfect mix of nature, fun, and relaxation. I highly recommend Ba Hồ for anyone visiting Nha Trang — it’s a must-see spot!

reviews.author: Anh Tran
reviews.text: Beautiful, untouched scenery, clear water, and fresh air, definitely worth a visit. However, the trail is rocky and can get quite slippery, especially when climbing to the second and third pools, so it’s best to wear grippy sports shoes for safety. Recommend heading up to the areas near Pool 1 or Pools 2 and 3, as they’re quieter and cleaner. The lower area near the Skywalk can get noisy and a bit dirty, as some local visitors enter without tickets and have loud picnics or drink there.

reviews.author: Daniela D
reviews.text: They include you mandatory canoe and skyline in the price, whether you use it or not price is same. But to use a small locker, you hace to pay extra.Buggy us also extra and unless you travel with disabled persons, it is not needed, path is pretty smooth and nice until the lakes. There is almost 0 work to keep somewhat accesible the path to lakes. Apart from some arrows painted on rocks and 2 ropes hanging from 2 rocks, there is no support to get there. Rocks are mostly super tall and unless you're above 1.80m tall, you'll have a super hard time to get to the lakes. And lakes are the nicest part really.I had to stop after 1st lake, as path to second lake turned into a 5m deep hole , super abrupt descent, vertical, with only one rope. Unless you really enjoy high risk if falling, adrenaline, descending and climbing vertical rocks by rope, you will arrive max at first lake if you are somewhat fit, with a huge hurdle and sweat. And one more thing for your safety. Don't come by bus, get a grab car even if you come from other place and need to wait more for a grab to take you. I was hit by a careless motorcycle on the way here, luckily enough I only got very bad bruises, and did not need to get medical help.

reviews.author: Grant Huber
reviews.text: Nice well kept natural area that’s easy access. Typical Vietnam, I felt very safe as they monitor the areas. The hike up to the 3 lakes is gorgeous, however, you need to be in good physical condition. Nobody told me about the little fish in the stream that nibble on you! I know where the aquarium fish come from that they use to nibble on your feet in tourist spots. Well worth the adventure but ring yours game for the hike.

reviews.author: NTDang
reviews.text: Bring swimwear. Would recommend good walking gear. Park itself is nice for a family or picknick day To reach waterfall 2 and 3 you have to do a bit of light climbing. I would have left my mom behind. For waterfall 3 prepare to get your feet wet. Forgot what the price was but I think it was reasonable. Plus they gave a bottle of water with the ticket.

reviews.author: Andrei Franco
reviews.text: The waterfall here is quite charming, though its appearance can vary depending on the season. There's an entrance fee to visit, but it includes access to a range of activities, making it a good value for the experience. You can enjoy kayaking, explore the area, or have a meal at one of the on-site restaurants. For a more relaxed option, picnicking is also a great choice. It’s a fantastic spot for a family day out, offering something for everyone to enjoy amidst the natural beauty.

reviews.author: Danny
reviews.text: Great landscape! But be aware that its not a family track at all but addults only!

reviews.author: Atlantic Beats
reviews.text: Highly recommend, we got there an hour and a half before they close the waterfall, it was raining a bit so we had the spot all to ourselves. Amazing

reviews.author: Tineke Rensen PowerEvents
reviews.text: If you think you can go to visit the waterfall in beautiful serene nature? That is not true. You have to pay entrance fee to a park with rollercoaster, cannopee tree tour etc. It is NOT possible to only visit the waterfall You pay 180.000 VND and you are only allowed to visit 1 of the 3 waterfalls. You need to pay more if you want to see the rest. The cannopee tour is included. Whether you want it or not Another place in Vietnam where nature is exploited by project developers

reviews.author: Aleksei
reviews.text: The walk through the park left a pleasant impression: the green trees and fresh air created an atmosphere of calm. We were especially impressed by the majestic waterfalls, the power of nature was mesmerizing. Unfortunately, due to the recent rain, the water was muddy. This was a bit disappointing, as we had planned to refresh ourselves in the cool water. Overall, the walk was a success, and we had a wonderful time in nature.

reviews.author: Kristina Ni
reviews.text: It is very good location and nice hiking and swimming spot! Please note that it might be challenging for small kids and senior people. Wear sport shoes and clothes and take a bottle of water to stay hydrated 🤍 also I recommend to plan more time for swimming in the lake #2 since it is very nice spot and the water is very comfortable temperature

reviews.author: Валерия Абт
reviews.text: The place has become a little tracking adventure for our relaxing vacation. It was great to make beautiful photos and to swim near the waterfall. P.S.: if you turn off the route, there is a greate chance to meet some wild monkeys. Be careful)

reviews.author: Tracy Danskin
reviews.text: One of the best swimming places I've been to. There are 3 swimming spots to visit and it's like an obstacle course to walk to them but well worth it. Amazing views and at the end you get to swim in the ice cool water. If you need a life jacket, they are supplied when you book with GET YOUR GUIDE. And wear walking shoes not thongs /flip flops/ jandles as there is a bit of climbing to do. Even swimming shoes are a good idea.

reviews.author: Antonija Magaš
reviews.text: The basic ticket to walk to the waterfalls seems a bit much (135k), but it's worth it. The place is beautiful and clean, you can easily spend half a day here chilling or playing in the lakes. The trail is short but very fun once you get to the river as it involves some scrambling over the boulders. I arrived around 9am on Sunday and there were barely any people there. Lake no. 2 was the busiest as that's where you can cliff jump. That's where most people seem to stop, too. Lake no. 3 and beyond were very peaceful. There's another lake just behind the "Don't go further" sign near lake no. 3. I had that lake just to myself the whole morning, other people started showing up only around noon. Showers are available near the entrance. The ticket includes a small bottle of water.

reviews.author: Petr Dumek
reviews.text: Really nice waterfalls and places to swim. 130k for person with "free" water. Also alot of toilets , trash bins and I saw even showers there. Ideal for spending half or full chill day when its hot outside.

reviews.author: Stanley Yang
reviews.text: Brought my wife and 2 kids there. (12yrs and 5 yrs) We arrived a 9am and stayed u til noon. It was a 10-12min walk inside the paved trails to the stream. From there we hiked up to both Lake number 1 and 2. Lake number 2 had the most shaded and rest areas. Parts of the climb was steep and needed some attention but my 5 yr old was a trooper. The lake area as great! Went for a swim and had little fishies nipping at your feet and body. Really well maintained and clean. Beautiful place. Great value at only 135k per person. My daughter got in free because she was below the height limit.

reviews.author: Kenny Ga
reviews.text: This place is amazing. Extremely easy to find. Rent a bike and drive the long way up the coast. 100k entry per person. Keep your parking ticket as they request it when you leave. The grounds are gorgeous and it’s a nice walk before you start climbing. Very slippery so bring good shoes and go very slow. Lots of handles and ropes make it easier. Water is not to cold and many places to take a dip. Not super crowded at all. We loved climbing around and hanging out. Just be careful and climb slowly!

reviews.author: Steffen Frank
reviews.text: Definitely worth a visit. We took a taxi to the venue and the driver waited until we were done. There weren't very many people, nice and quiet and beautiful nature. We didn't eat on site and the pool wasn't worth much, but the waterfall was and the walk itself was very beautiful. Good for medium-sized children. In the water there are small fish that nibble on the skin, which tickle quite a bit.

reviews.author: Éva Vancsó
reviews.text: Amazing and well-maintained park, climbing up to the lakes and waterfalls is adventurous but not too difficult. Swimming in a lake under a waterfall is a great experience. And if one do not want to climb, there are many places to just chill and enjoy the beauties of nature.

reviews.author: Aje
reviews.text: Nice falls in a beautiful jungle. Nature's lovers able to swim and climb will greatly enjoy the site. You will have to climb an iron trail across the boulders of the stream for 10 to 20 mn, depending on your shape, to fully appreciate the place. Recommended for flexible people in shape, knowing how to swim (the three pools are deep in some places). If not, you may be disappointed to spend 135 kd to stop before the first fall. This one can only be seen closely by swimming (get a nice water massage 🤗). Fifty meters further you reach the second pool but the fall is dangerous to play with. The best is the third, quite large (splinted) pool with a double fall you can play with. The 135 kd entry price includes insurance and a fresh water bottle, for 150 kd you get a round trip 3 mn (2 km) shuttle ride to and from the stream (otherwise it will cost 40 kd for the weary walker to call the shuttle for the way back). The 20 to 30 mn walk that follows starts gently and becomes gradually more acrobatic. The site opens at 08:00 and closes at 16:30. By the pools, there is a life guard with medical supplies, alert and other safety equipments. Suitable climbing shoes, back pack, usual tropical outdoor supplies and of course swimwear are advised.

reviews.author: Nikita wilson
reviews.text: Its about a 30minute hike in total and has 3 different waterfall pool spots with various things to do like swings, rock jumping ect at each. If one pool is too full you can always carry on and go to the next. Definitely don't recommend wearing flip-flops as there is abit of rock climbing involved with ropes so not for the faint hearted either

reviews.author: Queen B Jules
reviews.text: I went with a local guide, thank goodness I did cause I would have never figured out where to jump! It's about a 10 min walk to picnic areas and then another 15 rock climb to the waterfalls. The rocks are marked with arrows to guide you but if your planning on jumping I would HIGHLY RECCOMED having a guide show you. There's only a very small spot that you can jump into, cause just a few feet away is shallow rocks. If your adventurous it was awesome! Plus a guide can take photos for you!
